page: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors. Operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this dedicated hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of memory per core. The processor architecture incorporates a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark data highlights the server's exceptional multi-core processing and memory subsystem capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in multi-core CPU stress tests, PassMark CPU evaluations, and memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached read/write operations. It also demonstrates top-tier efficiency in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance and overall memory latency scores fall within the average tier, indicating that the system's strength lies in highly parallelized, multi-threaded execution rather than single-threaded tasks.

The c7i.metal-48xl does not include local NVMe storage, requiring external storage solutions for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and direct hardware access, making it highly efficient for workloads that can fully utilize its 192 vCPUs and specialized instruction sets, such as AMX for CPU-based deep learning inference.

This server is designed for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads. Suitable applications include large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning or LLM inference tasks, particularly those leveraging AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and features a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage, relying on network-attached storage instead. In benchmark testing, the c7i.metal-48xl delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput database and Redis operations. It features advanced hardware instruction sets, including Intel AMX and AVX-512, which significantly accelerate matrix multiplication and cryptographic tasks. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for compute-intensive, parallelized workloads such as large-scale database management, high-volume web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
